---
title: "Chapter 6: Comprehensions and Generators"
---

- Many programs are built around processing collections
    - e.g. lists, dictionary key, value pairs, sets
- *Comprehensions* are a special syntax for for quickly iterating and creating collections
    - They enhance readability
- *Generators* enable a continuous stream of values to be returned via a function
    - Generator results can be used anywhere a iterator is expected
    - Generators improve readability, improve performance, reduce memory usage, and simplify implementations
